page.title: Marbella Stag Do Yacht Budget: Real 2026 Breakdown

data.meta_description: 2026 marbella stag do yacht budget breakdown: €749 for 2h on an Astondoa 40 up to €4,719 for 4h on a Mangusta 80. Book now.
data.summary: A real 2026 cost breakdown for a marbella stag do yacht budget, covering Tier A and Tier B boats, inclusions, and tips to save without sacrificing the party.
key_facts: Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39): €749 for 2h, €2,299 for 8h
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80): €4,719 minimum 4h with free jet ski
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA
Maximum pax per boat: 5 (Dubhe) to 12 (Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, etc.)
All depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella
No hidden extras – price includes everything except tips and extra food
2026 rates are locked for bookings made in 2025

faq.q: What is the cheapest yacht for a stag do in Marbella?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 at €749 for 2h or €2,299 for 8h. Both depart from Puerto Banús, hold 9-11 pax, and include a skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. Perfect for a budget-conscious stag party.

faq.q: Can we bring our own alcohol on board?
faq.a: Yes, you can bring extra drinks. The charter already includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. Many stag groups bring spirits or mixers – just avoid glass bottles if possible, and no red wine (stains).

faq.q: How many people can join a stag yacht charter?
faq.a: It depends on the boat. The Dubhe (5 pax) is too small for a stag do. The Astondoa 40 (9 pax) and Azimut 39 (11 pax) work for smaller groups. For 12 pax, choose Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, Canados 86, Ferretti 94, or Maiora 26m.

faq.q: Is the Mangusta 80 worth the €4,719 for a stag do?
faq.a: If your group wants a 24m flybridge with a free jet ski, yes. The Mangusta 80 (white or grey) includes the jet ski, skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance. For 12 pax splitting the cost, it’s €393 per person for 4h – competitive for a luxury experience.

faq.q: What is included in the price? Is there VAT?
faq.a: Yes, Spanish IVA (21%) is included. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. No hidden fees.

faq.q: Can we swim and use the jet ski during the charter?
faq.a: Yes. The skipper will anchor at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for swimming. On Tier B charters (Mangusta 80), the jet ski is included and can be used under supervision. Always follow the skipper’s safety instructions.

faq.q: Do we need to pay a deposit? Is it refundable?
faq.a: Yes, a deposit is required to secure the booking. Cancellation policies vary – contact us via WhatsApp for the exact terms. For 2026 bookings made in 2025, rates are locked and no price increases apply.

faq.q: What time do charters start and end?
faq.a: Charters typically start at 10:00, 12:00, 14:00, or 16:00 from Puerto Banús. A 2h charter works for a quick party spin; 4h lets you swim and jet ski; 8h includes a lunch stop at a chiringuito. We’ll confirm exact times on booking.

data.body_html:
Planning a stag do in Marbella and wondering what a yacht actually costs in 2026? Here’s the real breakdown: no fluff, no hidden fees. Whether you’re splitting €749 for a 2-hour party on an Astondoa 40 or going all-in on a €4,719 Mangusta 80 with a jet ski, this guide gives you concrete numbers, boat options, and tips to stretch your marbella stag do yacht budget.

Marbella Stag Do Yacht Budget: Tier A vs Tier B

We split our fleet into two pricing tiers. Tier A covers smaller, fast boats perfect for a lively group. Tier B is for the big spenders who want a 24m flybridge and a free jet ski.

BoatLengthPaxPrice (2h)Price (8h)
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749€2,299
Azimut 3912.5m11€749€2,299
Mangusta 80 (any colour)24m12€4,719 minimum 4h

All other boats (Azimut 58, Sunseeker Predator 58, Ferretti 94, etc.) are priced on request via WhatsApp. No invented rates – just a quick message for a tailored quote.

What’s Included in Every Charter?

No surprises. Every booking includes:

  • Licensed skipper (English-speaking)
  • Fuel for the duration
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava
  • Light snacks (chips, olives, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%) – all taxes included

You can bring extra drinks, but avoid red wine (stains) and glass bottles if possible. The skipper will handle everything – just show up at Puerto Banús and party.

How Many People Can You Bring?

Capacity varies by boat. For a stag do, you want at least 8 pax to get the vibe going. Here’s the quick list:

  • 5 pax: Dubhe (too small for a stag do)
  • 9 pax: Astondoa 40
  • 10 pax: Lagoon 380, Pershing 46
  • 11 pax: Azimut 39, Sunseeker Predator 58
  • 12 pax: Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, Canados 86, Ferretti 94, K80, Maiora 26m

For a group of 12, the per-person cost on the Mangusta 80 is €393 for 4h – cheaper than a night out on the Golden Mile.

Where Will You Go?

All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s iconic marina. Typical routes include cruising past the Golden Mile, anchoring at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for swimming, and maybe a stop at a chiringuito for lunch (not included). The skipper knows the best spots for stag groups – calm waters, good photo ops, and a bit of privacy.

Tips to Save on Your Stag Do Yacht Budget

  • Book a 2-hour slot – €749 split between 9 guys = €83 each. Enough for a cruise, drinks, and photos.
  • Go for 4 hours on a Tier B boat – €4,719 for 12 pax = €393 each, including a jet ski. That’s a full afternoon of luxury.
  • Lock in 2025 prices – Book now for 2026 and rates won’t increase.
  • Bring your own spirits – The included drinks are enough, but adding your own whiskey or vodka saves bar prices.
  • Avoid peak weekends – Friday and Saturday in July/August are busiest. A Thursday or Sunday charter might get you a better slot.

Real Numbers: Example Stag Do Budget

Imagine 10 guys on the Azimut 39 for 4 hours (priced on request, but similar to Tier A). If the cost is €1,500 (estimate), that’s €150 per person. Add €50 for extra food and €20 for tips = €220 each. Compare that to a night out in Puerto Banús where a bottle service can cost €500+ per person – the yacht is actually better value.

For 12 guys on the Mangusta 80 for 4h at €4,719, per person is €393. That includes the jet ski – no extra rental fee.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the cheapest yacht for a stag do in Marbella?

The Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 at €749 for 2h or €2,299 for 8h. Both depart from Puerto Banús, hold 9-11 pax, and include a skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. Perfect for a budget-conscious stag party.

Can we bring our own alcohol on board?

Yes, you can bring extra drinks. The charter already includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. Many stag groups bring spirits or mixers – just avoid glass bottles if possible, and no red wine (stains).

How many people can join a stag yacht charter?

It depends on the boat. The Dubhe (5 pax) is too small for a stag do. The Astondoa 40 (9 pax) and Azimut 39 (11 pax) work for smaller groups. For 12 pax, choose Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, Canados 86, Ferretti 94, or Maiora 26m.

Is the Mangusta 80 worth the €4,719 for a stag do?

If your group wants a 24m flybridge with a free jet ski, yes. The Mangusta 80 (white or grey) includes the jet ski, skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance. For 12 pax splitting the cost, it’s €393 per person for 4h – competitive for a luxury experience.

What is included in the price? Is there VAT?

Yes, Spanish IVA (21%) is included. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. No hidden fees.

Can we swim and use the jet ski during the charter?

Yes. The skipper will anchor at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for swimming. On Tier B charters (Mangusta 80), the jet ski is included and can be used under supervision. Always follow the skipper’s safety instructions.

Do we need to pay a deposit? Is it refundable?

Yes, a deposit is required to secure the booking. Cancellation policies vary – contact us via WhatsApp for the exact terms. For 2026 bookings made in 2025, rates are locked and no price increases apply.

What time do charters start and end?

Charters typically start at 10:00, 12:00, 14:00, or 16:00 from Puerto Banús. A 2h charter works for a quick party spin; 4h lets you swim and jet ski; 8h includes a lunch stop at a chiringuito. We’ll confirm exact times on booking.

Ready to lock in your marbella stag do yacht budget for 2026? Browse our full fleet and book your charter now – no hidden fees, just a great party on the water.
